name: Doreen — 78, cares 40 hours a week for a friend on Attendance Allowance
note: The pension-age carer's classic: her £190 State Pension swallows the £86.45 rate, so Carer's Allowance pays nothing — and is still worth claiming, because the underlying entitlement adds £48.15 to her Pension Credit. The two cards teach it together.
checked: Overlap against gov.uk/carers-allowance/eligibility ('increased Pension Credit instead') and S.I. 1979/597; PC carer addition against SPC Regs reg 6(8). 2026-07-12.

carers-allowance.because: Entitled — but not paid: your State Pension is at least the £86.45 rate, and the two overlap. Claim anyway. An underlying entitlement is exactly what the Pension Credit carer question above means: it adds the £48.15 carer addition and raises the earnings disregard to £20 — in gov.uk's own words, people with higher pensions get increased Pension Credit instead. And unpaid entitlement does not touch the cared-for person's own benefits.
